title: Make citations verifiable
description: Each citation points to a specific retrievable passage with enough locator that a user or test can confirm it supports the claim.
body: Use when the answer presents citations. Do not emit a bare document title as a citation, and avoid citations that point to a passage that does not actually contain the claim. Actions: attach a stable passage id or span locator to each citation, ensure the cited passage was in the retrieved set, and verify it supports the specific claim. Validation signals include a test that resolves each citation to its passage and checks the claim is supported by that text. Risk boundary: a citation that does not support its claim is worse than none because it fabricates trust; treat unsupported citations as a blocking quality finding.
